item.text: I’ll read the vulnerability note and inspect the referenced code paths before editing, then keep the patch narrowly scoped to the reported issue.

item.aggregated_output:
Project: appium/appium-mcp
Vulnerability type: Cross-site Scripting (CWE-79)

Where the vulnerability is (file -> vulnerable line ranges, with enclosing function):

  src/ui/mcp-ui-utils.ts
    - lines 720-743   in: export function createLocatorGeneratorUI(
    - lines 895-900   in: export function createLocatorGeneratorUI(
    - lines 1633-1635   in: export function addUIResourceToResponse(

item.text: The finding points to UI HTML/resource generation in `src/ui/mcp-ui-utils.ts`. I’m reading those sections plus nearby helpers to see how values are currently escaped and transported into the UI.

item.aggregated_output: `; } /** * Helper function to add UI resource to response content * Returns both text and UI resource for backward compatibility */ export function addUIResourceToResponse( response: { content: Array<{ type: string; text?: string }> }, uiResource: ReturnType ): { content: Array } { if (process.env.NO_UI === 'true' || process.env.NO_UI === '1') { return response; } return { content: [...response.content, uiResource], }; }

item.text: The vulnerable output is direct interpolation of locator metadata into HTML and an inline event handler. I’m checking the resource wrapper as well, since the finding calls out response attachment and that may need a boundary check rather than just escaping individual fields.

item.aggregated_output:
{
  "name": "appium-mcp",
  "mcpName": "io.github.appium/appium-mcp",
  "version": "1.85.9",
  "type": "module",
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "git+https://github.com/appium/appium-mcp.git"
  },
  "bugs": {
    "url": "https://github.com/appium/appium-mcp/issues"
  },
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/index.js"
    },
    "./core": {
      "types": "./dist/core.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/core.js"
    },
    "./package.json": "./package.json"
  },
  "bin": {
    "appium-mcp": "dist/index.js"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "build": "rimraf dist tsconfig.tsbuildinfo && tsc -b && chmod +x dist/index.js",
    "start": "node dist/index.js",
    "start:stdio": "node dist/index.js",
    "start:httpStream": "node dist/index.js --httpStream",
    "start:httpStream:port": "node dist/index.js --httpStream --port=",
    "telemetry:jaeger:start": "sh tools/telemetry/jaeger.sh start",
    "telemetry:jaeger:stop": "sh tools/telemetry/jaeger.sh stop",
    "telemetry:jaeger:logs": "sh tools/telemetry/jaeger.sh logs",
    "telemetry:jaeger:config": "sh tools/telemetry/jaeger.sh config",
    "telemetry:appium:start:httpStream": "APPIUM_MCP_OTEL_ENABLED=true OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=appium-mcp-local OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT=http://127.0.0.1:4318/v1/traces OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER=parentbased_always_on npm run start:httpStream",
    "dev": "npx fastmcp dev src/index.js",
    "inspect": "npx fastmcp inspect src/index.js",
    "dev:built": "node dist/index.js",
    "inspect:built": "npx @modelcontextprotocol/inspector node dist/index.js",
    "verify:names": "node scripts/verify-names.mjs",
    "test": "NODE_OPTIONS=--experimental-vm-modules jest",
    "test:locators": "NODE_OPTIONS=--experimental-vm-modules jest src/tests/generate-all-locators.test.ts",
    "lint": "eslint src --ext .ts",
    "lint:fix": "eslint src --ext .ts --fix",
    "format": "prettier --write \"src/**/*.{ts,js,json}\"",
    "format:check": "prettier --check \"src/**/*.{ts,js,json}\"",
    "check": "npm run lint && npm run format:check",
    "prepublishOnly": "npm run build",
    "sync-version": "node scripts/sync-version.mjs",
    "version": "npm run sync-version"
  },
  "author": "",
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "description": "Intelligent MCP server providing AI assistants with powerful tools and resources for Appium mobile automation",
  "dependencies": {

item.text: There is no shared HTML escaping helper in this file; page-source and code views do local escaping. I’m going to add small context-specific encoders and remove inline JavaScript argument interpolation from the locator rows by using `data-*` attributes and a delegated click handler.

I found the main exploitable path: untrusted page-source attributes become locator fields, then render as raw HTML and inline handler arguments. The patch will keep the visible UI and tool call the same, but encode displayed values and move selector data out of executable JavaScript.

I’m applying the fix in the UI utility module and adding regression tests that use payloads like closing tags, inline event attributes, backticks, and script tags so the vulnerable interpolation pattern is covered directly.

The locator UI now escapes every untrusted field before rendering and uses data attributes plus a nonce-protected script for the Test action. I’m running the targeted tests and TypeScript build next to catch syntax or module issues.

item.text: One additional gap from the finding is the response attachment boundary. I’m adding a minimal runtime shape check there so `addUIResourceToResponse` only appends the expected MCP HTML resource shape, while preserving normal callers.
I’m making the response helper defensive against malformed UI resources, which limits that sink without changing successful UI responses created by `createUIResource`.
I noticed the runtime guard should also tolerate completely malformed values passed through `any`, so I’m tightening that check and extending the regression test for the attachment helper.
The patch context crossed files, so I’m applying the guard and test update separately.

The source edits are in place. I’m doing a final diff review now, with attention to whether the guard changes normal behavior and whether the locator UI still posts the same `appium_find_element` payload.

item.text: The diff review showed one lint-risk pattern: the runtime guard referenced `createUIResource` before its declaration in a type position. I’m moving that helper below `createUIResource` to stay aligned with stricter lint configs.
I’m cleaning up the test formatting in the one long assertion now. After that I’ll rerun the available checks, expecting the same missing-dependency blocker unless dependencies are present.
The patch is ready for verification. I’m rerunning the targeted test and build commands once more so the final status reflects the current workspace.

item.text: The final source review is clean at the patch level. I’m running a whitespace check, which does not need project dependencies, to catch accidental trailing spaces or malformed patch output.

Implemented the XSS remediation in [src/ui/mcp-ui-utils.ts](/work/src/ui/mcp-ui-utils.ts):

- Escapes locator UI fields before inserting them into HTML.
- Removed inline `onclick` interpolation for locator testing and switched to `data-*` attributes plus delegated click handling.
- Added a nonce-backed CSP for the locator generator UI script.
- Added a defensive shape check before `addUIResourceToResponse` appends UI resources.
- Added regression coverage in [src/tests/ui-mcp-utils.test.ts](/work/src/tests/ui-mcp-utils.test.ts).

Verification:
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/tests/ui-mcp-utils.test.ts` could not run because `jest` is not installed.
- `npm run build` could not run because `rimraf` is not installed.

No commits or non-local project changes were made.
